claim_text: IEA says wait times for critical grid components such as transformers and cables have doubled in the past three years.
notes: Supports grid components as a bottleneck for AI-related load growth.

claim_text: IEA says procurement now takes up to four years for large power transformers and two to three years for cables, with average lead times almost doubled since 2021.
notes: Strong support for transmission equipment bottlenecks.

claim_text: NEMA said transformer lead times had reached a current average of 120 weeks in September 2024.
notes: Important U.S.-specific confirmation of severe lead times.

claim_text: DOE's large power transformer resilience report says 36-month lead times are commonly quoted and maximum lead times reach 60 months.
notes: Strong support that large power transformers are the most acute transformer bottleneck.

claim_text: DOE says over 60 million distribution transformers are in service in the United States.
notes: Shows the breadth and installed-base importance of the distribution-transformer layer.

claim_text: DOE says distribution transformers are increasingly needed for EV chargers and NREL projects a tripling of distribution transformer installations by 2050.
notes: Strong support for long-duration growth in distribution-transformer demand.

claim_text: DOE says distribution transformers serve homes, businesses, manufacturing, and data centers, and researchers estimate about 55 percent of in-service units are older than 33 years.
notes: Supports replacement demand plus growth demand.

claim_text: DOE's FITT program was launched to improve grid reliability and ease transformer supply chain constraints.
notes: Government policy directly acknowledges transformer supply constraints.

claim_text: DOE's 2024 data-center energy-use report says U.S. data-center load is projected to double or triple by 2028.
notes: Supports the demand-side pressure on grid infrastructure.

claim_text: Cables and related transmission components are also supply constrained and can take two to three years to procure, with some DC cables beyond five years.
notes: Supports cables as a meaningful adjacent bottleneck.

claim_text: Distribution transformers are currently a more severe bottleneck than large power transformers.
notes: Evidence points more strongly to utility-scale power transformers as the more acute chokepoint.

claim_text: Cables are already a clearly bigger bottleneck than transformers.
notes: Evidence supports cable constraints, but not yet that they dominate transformer constraints.
